observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate and LGSO
submit written justification for the above noted deficiencies.

1.11. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate reconcile
his PPE records and provide the LGSO with the detailed reconciled balance
as basis for the physical count.

1.12. We further recommended that the LGSO upon submission by the Municipal
Accountant of the reconciled balance, trace back previous records, prepare
correct Property Cards with the necessary details for all properties especially
the accountability portion, provide Property Acknowledgement Receipt
(PAR) for previously procured equipment that were not issued with PAR and
update issued PAR that existed more than 3 years.

1.13. Lastly, we recommended that the new Inventory Committee conduct
Physical Count of the PPEs and submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team on
or before May 31, 2023.

2.8. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
adjustments for the above erroneous entries and record the corresponding
expense for the issued inventories once supported with RIS and ICS by the
LGSO.

3.14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in the preparation of
succeeding Financial Statements, shall classify the current portion of Loans
Payable- Domestic as Current Liabilities and shall provide detailed
information as to the composition of each loan account in the Notes to the
Financial Statement.

3.15. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the root cause of the
unreconciled variance in the Loans Payable – Domestic account balance and
make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the same and to reflect the
correct balance of the same.

4.12. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate:
a.) submit an explanation to the Audit Team why Bank Reconciliation
Statement was not prepared for the General Fund-Main period from July
to December 2022;
b.) fast track to verify with PNB the existence of the amount of ₱35,989.90 in
the SEF-PNB account and prepare the necessary correcting entry; and
c.) prepare necessary Journal Entry Vouchers to record the valid book
reconciling items per Bank Reconciliation Statements on the month that
they occurred or were detected in order to present an accurate and
reliable cash balance in the financial statements.

5.13. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal
Development Council identify and include in the AIP specific priority
projects which are well-planned and ready for implementation in the current
budget year.

5.14. We also recommended that the:
a.) Municipal Mayor submit justification/explanation why the expenses were
incurred or contracts entered without the requisite SB Resolution
authorizing the same; and
b.) Municipal Accountant submit justification/explanation why the
disbursements were approved despite incompleteness of supporting
documents due to lack of SB Resolution authorizing the same.

6.10. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the MDRRM
Council, MDRRM Officer and Municipal Budget Officer:
a.) submit justification and provide a legal basis that will allow the
charging of a patrol vehicle to the LDRRMF; and
b.) henceforth, adhere with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular
No. 2014-155 dated December 17, 2014 and DBM Budget Circular No.
2017-1 dated April 26, 2017 on the acquisition and use of government
motor vehicles.

7.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the MDRRM
Council, and the MDRRM Officer:
a.) maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF in order to effectively
increase the level of preparedness of the municipality during natural
calamities.
b.) include in the annual LDRRMF Investment Plan the programs,
projects and activities to be funded from the prior years’ unexpended
LDRRM Fund to ensure that its utilization is well-planned and duly
authorized by the MDRRM Council.

8.7. We recommended that the Local DRRM Officer prepare and submit a duly
certified Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Funds on or before the
15th day after the end of each month, to facilitate monitoring on the proper
utilization of the funds and implementation of PPAs that could help
enhanced their capability to respond to disaster.

9.8. We recommended that the Person-in-Charge (Mayor’s Office) and MSWDO
submit justification to the Audit Team on the noted variances or
lack/incompleteness of distribution reports and the unaccounted stockpile at
yearend.

9.9. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the
submission of complete Distribution Report with summarized report before
drawing entry for expense.

10.11 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Agriculturist
submit justification on the non-submission of actual distribution/utilization
of the procured items totaling ₱910,230.00.

10.12 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate refrain
from drawing expense for inventories for distribution if not supported with
distribution list to end users with quantity and amount equivalent to the
expense drawn.

10.13 We further recommended that the person in charge (Municipal Agriculture
Office) of the distribution/utilization of the above-procured items, submit to
the Audit Team the lacking distribution/utilization reports.

11.12. We recommended that the LGSO present the unaccounted various medical
equipment costing ₱10,873,025.00, otherwise, submit a justification why they
could not be located/presented and properly accounted.

11.13. We also recommended that the LGSO prepare and issue ARE or Property
Acknowledgement Receipt to the MHO for the Medical Equipment totaling
₱185,000.00.

11.14. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor initiate the refund of
₱10,873,025.00 if the subject equipment could not be presented and/or
accounted.

12.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all Department Heads
or immediate supervisors to verify before signing the DTRs of their
subordinates by referring to the logbooks, which should be completely filled
up as to time in and out, to ensure that actual attendance and work are
indeed rendered by the regular employees and job order personnel.

12.10. We also recommended that the HRMO and Municipal Accountant check the
completeness of the DTRs, including copies of the logbooks, attached to the
payrolls.

12.11. Finally, we recommended that the management bring back the use of
biometrics system for recording attendance to create a more reliable
timekeeping record.

13.18 We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer record all the
donations-in-kind received by the RHU in the Summary/List of Donations
Received, Distributed and Balances, duly supported with the (1)
Acknowledgment Receipts, (2) proof of receipt by and distribution to the
beneficiaries not necessarily in the required form, and (3) the inventory of
the remaining undistributed items, if any; and submit a copy of the same to
the Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant.

13.19 We also recommended that the Supply Officer, assist the Municipal Health
Officer in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-in-kind, and in the
conduct of inventory of the remaining undistributed items, if any.

13.20 We further recommended that the Supply Officer maintain copies of the
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and its
supporting documents and prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated
Relief Goods for DRRM.

13.21 Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, based on the
Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM, make a disclosure in the
Notes to Financial Statement as to quantity received, issued and balance of
all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality, with categorization as to
food items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits and others.

14.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor strictly require the Revenue
Collectors to remit collections intact daily or the next working day.

14.9. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the collections
daily or as soon as collections reach ₱10,000.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee to (1) conduct a complete physical count of LGU properties, with the assistance of the Municipal Assessor (for identification of LGU-owned land and buildings) and the Municipal Engineer (for identification of on-going projects), and (2) to prepare a complete RCPPE and (3) submit this to the Municipal Accountant Designate on or before June 30, 2021.

(4) We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate and the Designated General Service Officer reconcile their respective records, (5) verify any discrepancies noted and (6) prepare the necessary adjustments to present the accurate PPE account balances.

(15) We recommended that the Supply and Property Custodian should strictly issue an Inventory Custodian Slips for small, tangible and non-consumable items costing ₱15,000.00 or less, that could not be considered as PPE but has a life of more than one year.

(30) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the provision of an adequate and proper permanent storage area for all welfare goods donated and purchased by the Municipal Government to ensure that the items are safeguarded against loss and wastage.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant- Designate, in the preparation of succeeding Financial Statements, (34) shall classify the current portion of Loans Payable- Domestic as Current Liabilities and (35) shall provide detailed information as to the composition of each loan account in the Notes to the Financial Statement.

We recommended that the Designated MDRRM Officer (36) shall integrate in a single LDRRMF Investment Plan the projects, programs and activities to be funded from the unexpended Special Trust Fund and the current appropriations of the 70% Mitigation Fund.
(37) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall recommended to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval any proposed revisions on the Tax Ordinance and implement the same; and

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipality’s HRMO, shall:
(38) fill up vacant positions in the LGU plantilla in accordance with CSC rules and regulations, subject to Personnel Service limitation;

(39) pay the mandated daily minimum wage prescribed by the RTWPB for Region VIII; and

(40) reduce the number of Job Order Workers so that the functions of the existing regular employees are not duplicated by the JOs.

(41) We recommended that the Municipal SWM Board, through the Municipal Mayor as the Chairman shall incorporate in the SWM Plan provision for attendance of qualified LGU personnel in necessary trainings, preferably those conducted by TESDA, who shall cascade his learnings to other personnel involved in the collection of solid wastes.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant- Designate (42) prepare lapsing schedules for each infrastructure projects to determine the correct Accumulated Depreciation as of December 31, 2018 and (43) prepare the necessary correcting entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer: (44) conduct an updated inventory of all road projects of the LGU and (45) prepare a report thereon, including the complete description and cost segregation of road components for each road project, and (46) provide the Designated Municipal Accountant and the Head of the General Services Office with the Inventory Report.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal General Services Officer-Designate (47) prepare and maintain complete Local Road Network Property Card for all the municipal roads and the Road Map of the LGU.

(48) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and the Solid Waste Management Officer- Designate, specifically require all barangays to segregate solid wastes and monitor closely the barangay’s compliance of its implementation. The ABC thru DILG is advised to undergo trainings and seminars for MSWM in Barangay level.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate (49) prepare and file the necessary documents for the registration of Mahaplag Inland Resort Hotel; (50) compute the taxes due to the National Government from the operations of MIRH; (51) file the appropriate returns and pay the taxes due immediately and; (52) file the returns and pay the taxes due in the succeeding periods.   Any penalties and surcharges should be paid by the employees liable for the late payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer (53) prepare the separate Operating Budget for the Mahaplag Inland Resort and Hotel and coordinate with the SB for its approval.  Also, we recommended that (54) all expenses pertaining to Mahaplag Inland Resort and Hotel, including the Personal Services, be sourced from the income generated in the Resort.

Further, we recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan (55) revisit and evaluate the rates approved in 2011 as payment for the services offered in Mahaplag Inland Resort and Hotel to increase the income of the Resort.

(56) We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deputize the Barangay Treasurers, in addition to the collectors of the LGU to collect all taxes on real property located in the Barangay, provided that the Barangay Treasurer is properly bonded for the purpose. Collection and remittance of RPT by Barangay Treasurer be closely monitored by the Municipal Treasurer.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the detailed DILG LGOO (57) refund immediately the Representation Allowance received in CY 2017.
(58) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require each program and project coordinator to submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents to the Source Agency immediately.

This is a reiterated finding, thus we repeatedly recommend (59) strict compliance with provisions of the law and rules governing cash advances. We reiterate compliance with the hereunder course of actions to wit:                                                                          

 (60) Strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as well as other related pertinent rules and regulations; and             

(61) Immediate submission to the audit team for audit of all original required documents on the settlement and liquidations of all accountable officers.                                                            

(62) Further, we recommend that the LGU request authority from COA before salary deductions on instalment basis be applied.

(63) prudence in the utilization and use of government funds be exercised and observed at all times by management to prevent incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, extravagant and unconscionable expenditures.

We recommend that management should (64) secure a copy of the purchaser’s permit from the National Telecommunication Commission and (65) a dealer’s permit of the supplier issued also by the NTC to consider the transaction of purchasing Radio Handheld Transceivers, Systems and Accessories a valid and legitimate expenditure. Such supporting document is necessary to prevent transaction from being questioned in audit.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should (66) ensure that all concerned officials and employees of the municipality to faithfully discharge their duties and functions for the submission of paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, collections receipts, deposits and its supporting documents within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor revisit the previously issued Executive Order on the creation of the Inventory Committee issued in CY 2015 and reconstitute the Committee, if needed.  The committee should give priority to the conduct of complete Inventory Count of PPEs every end of the year and prepare the Report containing the result of the count of all property of the LGU and submit to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of the following year.

Also, we recommended that the General Services Officer actively perform her duties and responsibilities, as stated in Article XX, Section 490 of RA 7160, including the maintenance of updated and complete records of all PPE of the Municipality.

Once inventory count is made and Inventory Report is prepared, we recommended that the GSO Designate update her property records based on the report and reconcile the property records with the accounting records to come up with the reconciled balances of PPE accounts, which will be considered the correct balance of the agency's PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record drugs and medicines purchased for distribution as inventory and recognize the corresponding expense based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (Drugs and Medicines) prepared by the Municipal Health Office duly supported with properly filled-out Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS).

We also recommended that the Inventory Committee shall conduct a physical count of inventories every end of the semester and prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant every July 10 and January 10 of every year.

We also recommended that the Municipal Health Officer, or the personnel in-charge in the distribution of Drugs and Medicines, to submit every end of the month the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) supported with duly filled-out Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) to the Municipal Accountant which will be the basis to record the cost of the drugs and medicines expenses during the month.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the uncollected stall rentals as of December 31, 2021 to set the accounts in order.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the Manager of the Waterworks System to submit to the Audit Team why disconnection of services was not made on delinquent water consumers despite accumulated bills as discussed in paragraph numbers 3.6 and 3.7; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit explanation to the Audit Team on the accumulated uncollected water bills.

We further recommended that, henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and the Manager of the Waterworks System, furnish the Municipal Accountant with the monthly Billing Statements of stall lessees and water system consumers so that the pertinent Income and Receivables will be recorded in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant communicate with the Provincial Accounting Officer to inquire the declared value of the mini rescue vehicle.  Once amount is determined, recognize in the books the donated Rescue Vehicle, including the depreciation in CY 2021.

We also recommended that the General Services Officer:
transfer the ownership per LTO Records from Provincial Government of Leyte to the Municipal Government of Mahaplag; and
immediately issue PAR to the end user of the Mini Rescue Vehicle.  Moving forward, PAR should be immediately issued once properties are released to accountable officers/ end users.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.    make the necessary journal entries to transfer the LGU counterpart or Equity to the Trust Fund Books, as discussed in paragraph numbers 5.4 and 5.5;
b.   henceforth, record the transactions pertaining to Fund Transfers in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the LGU counterpart or equity share of P403,239.94 from the General Fund depository account to the Trust Fund depository account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant Designate:
issue Demand Letters to Barangays with outstanding unliquidated Fund Transfers;
upon receipt of the liquidation documents from the Barangay Treasurer, prepare and attach certification that funds previously transferred to the Implementing Agency (IA) has been liquidated, post audited and accounted for in the books before signifying in the Disbursement Voucher the propriety and completeness of supporting documents.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop issuing checks for Financial Assistance to Barangays with unliquidated Fund Transfers.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the account Welfare Goods for Distribution in recording relief goods for distribution and the correct expense account upon issuance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the Municipal Accountant to submit explanation for granting Cash Advances to employees with unliquidated cash advances as presented in Table 11;
order the withholding of the payment of any money due to the employees with unliquidated cash advances; and
file a request for write off with the Commission on Audit of unliquidated cash advances of deceased employees based on the list of the Municipal Accountant duly supported with Death Certificate.

We also recommended that, henceforth, avoid granting cash advance to employees who have unliquidated cash advance.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary correcting entries for the noted errors in the recording of cash advances granted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary documents needed to turn-over the Road Concreting in Sitio Beginning, Barangay Polahongon.  Once the projects are turned over, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to remove the said assets from the books of accounts of LGU Mahaplag.
Moving forward, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the correct accounts in recording receipt of materials from supplier, implementation and completion of the project and eventually, transfer of completed project to the recipient barangay.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate submit explanation to the Audit Team why Bank Reconciliation Statement was not prepared for the General Fund-Main;
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify with PNB the existence of the amount of P35,989.90 in SEF-PNB account and prepare necessary correcting entry.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make correcting entry on the above erroneous recording.

We recommended that the MDRRMO submit explanation to the Audit Team why the Local DRRMF Investment Plan was not prepared and the DRRM Fund was not utilized.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor and MDRRMO identify programs and projects to be funded by the Trust Liability – DRRM and include them in the Local DRRMF Investment Plan so that the unexpended balances will be utilized in accordance with its purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer open an account with a government depository bank for the DRRMF-Trust Fund and transfer all unexpended LDRRMF within the last 5 years to said bank account.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer to conform with COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up and maintain Special Accounts in the GF and prepare all the required financial reports for the LEEs of the LGU for monitoring of the operating performance and viability of these LEEs and to serve as a decision-making tool of the agency. Transactions pertaining to an economic enterprise should be recorded in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers.
We also recommended that the LEE Manager (personnel in-charge) prepare the required 5-year business plan for each LEE.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Finance Committee conduct the required Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review and submit and present the same to the Municipal Mayor and local sanggunian.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the payment of additional compensation to National Government officials or employees who are not entitled to the same as per Compensation Policy Guidelines 98-01. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary journal entries to correct the noted error in the recording of payment to Job Order.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the head of the Local Development Council:
avoid using generic terms in the LGU’s Annual and Supplemental Budgets, instead, specify the projects to be undertaken in a budget year.
obtain Local Sanggunian authorization prior to entering contract for PPAs with generic appropriation.
We also recommended that Municipal Budget Officer stop charging obligations or disbursements which are unrelated to the existing appropriations.  This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Engineer implement projects by administration only when the LGU meets all the requirements stated in GPPB 18-2006.  This observation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer submit immediately the lacking supporting documents to establish propriety of the payments made.
We also recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that the required supporting documents of all claims are complete before affixing his signature in Box A of the disbursement voucher to ensure that only valid and proper claims are approved for payment.
We recommended that the GSO prepare property tags which shall contain the information enumerated in Section 5.7 of COA Circular 2020-006 and attach the same to the PPEs.

(7) We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate prepare the Disbursement Voucher and other necessary supporting documents to transfer back to the General Fund the unutilized 70% Mitigation Fund – CO of ₱232,536.00 from Special Trust Fund.

(8) We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their records to exactly determine the unexpended balances and the amount needed to be transferred to the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund.

(9) We recommended that the Local Sanggunian of Mahaplag, Leyte strictly adhere to the rules and regulations provided in the guidelines of all grants received and strictly review the appropriation enacted to the actual amount received/ available.

(10) We recommended that the Municipal Accountant- Designate should ensure that the required necessary documents are complete before certifying in the Disbursement Voucher to avoid suspension in audit.

(11) We also recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer should stop accepting expired collection receipts for payments made by the LGU not just for diesel and gasoline but for all goods and services availed and to be paid.

(12) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant-Designate should submit detailed distribution lists for all the relief goods distributed sourced from BGCM Fund. Such distribution lists must be duly signed by the beneficiaries with details on the dates of distribution, items received, and quantity of each items received supported with pictures during the distribution.

(13) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Chairman of the Municipal Development Council, Municipal Budget Officer and Designated Municipal Accountant stop utilizing grants received other than their intended purpose and (14) submit to the Audit Team justification for utilizing COVID fund for other purposes.

(16) We recommended that the municipal officials should appropriate the amount of ₱137,068.65 to pay for the remaining unpaid loan amortization as of December 31, 2020.

(17) We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate and the Municipal Treasurer should communicate in writing to the MDFO regarding the existing loan balances and a schedule of loan amortizations for all Sub-Project Loans of the LGU in CY 2021 which will be the basis in preparing the Supplemental Budgets and submit to the Sanggunian Bayan for approval.

(18) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and Designated - Municipal Accountant and all concerned LGU employees should settle the ballooning loan penalties with MDFO.

(19) We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor should send a representative who will discuss with the MDFO regarding the status and settlement of loan penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should:

(20) issue a directive to the Punong Barangays who were recipients of the materials donated in CY 2020 to submit a report on its utilization including proof of the completed project; and

(21) execute a MOA with the Punong Barangay who is a recipient of donated materials from the LGU, stating therein the submission of proof of utilization of the materials given for the intended project within a specific period of time.

(22) We also recommended that the designated Municipal Accountant record the cost of materials transferred to the Barangays under Due from Other LGU account so as to monitor compliance of the recipient barangays with the submission of the Utilization Reports.

(23) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Chairman of the LDRRMC, LDRRM Officer-Designate and Municipal Budget Officer should utilize the LDRRM Fund for activities related to the disaster reduction, preparedness and mitigation measures only.

(24) We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should stop accepting expired collection receipts for payments made by the LGU not just for diesel and gasoline but for all goods and services availed and to be paid.

(25) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the assistance of the Designated-LDRRM Officer, submit detailed distribution lists for all the relief goods distributed sourced from LDRRMF. Such distribution lists must be duly signed by the beneficiaries with details on the dates of distribution, items received, and quantity of each items received supported with pictures during the distribution.

(26) We also recommended that the Designated-LDRRM Officer should conduct a physical count of all the undistributed items and (27)a Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) should be prepared and submitted to the Designated Municipal Accountant.

(28) We recommended further that the Designated Municipal Accountant should record in the books the undistributed welfare goods, if these are procured by the LGU.  (29) For those items received from donors, these shall be recorded in the registry.

(31) We recommended that the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF using the format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 should be prepared monthly and (32) certified correct by the Local Accountant and (33) submitted by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Audit Team.

We recommend for management to (67) strictly submit on the prescribed time all perfected purchase orders/ contracts as well as IARs. This shall be complied with to facilitate releases of funds solely to valid transactions.

1.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee
to conduct a complete physical count of LGU properties, with the assistance
of the Municipal Assessor (for identification of LGU-owned land and
buildings) and the Municipal Engineer (for identification of on-going
projects), and to prepare a complete RCPPE and submit this to the
Municipal Accountant Designate on or before June 30, 2021.
1.10 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate and the
Designated General Service Officer reconcile their respective records, verify
any discrepancies noted and prepare the necessary adjustments to present
the accurate PPE account balances.
1.11 In the Exit Conference, the concerned officials informed the Audit Team that the
observation is well taken and assured immediate and full compliance to the
recommendations given.

2.11. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate prepare the
Disbursement Voucher and other necessary supporting documents to
transfer back to the General Fund the unutilized 70% Mitigation Fund – CO
of ₱232,536.00 from Special Trust Fund.
2.12. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the
Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their records to exactly determine the
unexpended balances and the amount needed to be transferred to the
LDRRMF Special Trust Fund.
2.13. During the Exit Conference held on May 27, 2021, the Municipal Accountant –
Designate promised to prepare the DV with complete supporting documents for
the transfer. Reconciliation of records for succeeding transfers was also
guaranteed by the Municipal Accountant - Designate together with the Municipal
Budget Officer.

3.9. We recommended that the Local Sanggunian of Mahaplag, Leyte strictly
adhere to the rules and regulations provided in the guidelines of all grants
received and strictly review the appropriation enacted to the actual amount
received/ available.
3.10. Strict adherence to the rules and regulations was assured by the officials
concerned during the Exit Conference on May 27, 2021.

4.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant- Designate should ensure
that the required necessary documents are complete before certifying in the
Disbursement Voucher to avoid suspension in audit.
4.11. We also recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer should stop
accepting expired collection receipts for payments made by the LGU not just
for diesel and gasoline but for all goods and services availed and to be paid.
4.12. In a letter reply to the recommendations, the Municipal Accountant – Designate
and Acting Municipal Treasurer promised to adhere to the recommendations.

5.8. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant-
Designate should submit detailed distribution lists for all the relief goods
distributed sourced from BGCM Fund. Such distribution lists must be duly
signed by the beneficiaries with details on the dates of distribution, items
received and quantity of each items received supported with pictures during
the distribution.
5.9. In a letter from the Municipal Mayor and Accountant – Designate, as reply to the
Audit Observation, they assured the Audit Team that they will adhere to the
recommendations and follow the same documentations for LGU’s future
distributions.

7.5. We recommended that the Supply and Property Custodian should strictly
issue an Inventory Custodian Slips for small, tangible and non-consumable
items costing ₱15,000.00 or less, that could not be considered as PPE but has
a life of more than one year.
7.6. During the conducted Exit Conference, the Supply and Property Custodian
ensured the Audit Team the issuance of ICS for all small, tangible and non-
consumable items already procured and to be procured by the LGU.

8.11. We recommended that the municipal officials should appropriate the amount
of ₱137,068.65 to pay for the remaining unpaid loan amortization as of
December 31, 2020.
8.12. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate and the
Municipal Treasurer should communicate in writing to the MDFO regarding
the existing loan balances and a schedule of loan amortizations for all Sub-
Project Loans of the LGU in CY 2021 which will be the basis in preparing the
Supplemental Budgets and submit to the Sanggunian Bayan for approval.
8.13. In the Exit Conference held on May 27, 2021, the Municipal Budget Officer gave
the assurance to appropriate the amount ₱137,068.65 for the unpaid loan
amortization. The concerned officials also promised to earnestly coordinate with
MDFO regarding the LGU’s loans.

9.7. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer,
Municipal Budget Officer and Designated - Municipal Accountant and all
concerned LGU employees should settle the ballooning loan penalties with
MDFO.
9.8. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor should send a
representative who will discuss with the MDFO regarding the status and
settlement of loan penalties.
9.9. Immediate adherence to the audit recommendations while observing due diligence
was promised by the mentioned LGU officials during the Exit Conference.

11.10. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as Chairman of the LDRRMC,
LDRRM Officer-Designate and Municipal Budget Officer should utilize the
LDRRM Fund for activities related to the disaster reduction, preparedness
and mitigation measures only.
11.11. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should stop accepting
expired collection receipts for payments made by the LGU not just for diesel
and gasoline but for all goods and services availed and to be paid.
11.12. The concerned officials promised to adhere to the audit recommendations given.

12.14. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor with the assistance of the
Designated-LDRRM Officer, submit detailed distribution lists for all the
relief goods distributed sourced from LDRRMF. Such distribution lists must
be duly signed by the beneficiaries with details on the dates of distribution,
items received and quantity of each items received supported with pictures
during the distribution.
12.15. We also recommended that the Designated-LDRRM Officer should conduct
a physical count of all the undistributed items and a Report on Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI) should be prepared and submitted to the
Designated Municipal Accountant.
12.16. We recommended further that the Designated Municipal Accountant should
record in the books the undistributed welfare goods, if these are procured by
the LGU. For those items received from donors, these shall be recorded in
the registry.
12.17. During the Exit Conference, concerned officials assured the Audit Team that
immediate compliance to the given recommendations will be observed with
utmost diligence.

13.9. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the provision of an
adequate and proper permanent storage area for all welfare goods donated
and purchased by the Municipal Government to ensure that the items are
safeguarded against loss and wastage.
13.10. In the Exit Conference, the Municipal Mayor is amenable to the recommendation
and promised to comply with the assistance of the LGU officials especially the
Municipal Engineer.

14.3. For CY 2020, the Local Government of Mahaplag has a total LDRRMF of
₱13,641,889.07. Table 20 shows the breakdown of LDRRMF.
14.4. During the post-audit of LDRRMF, the Municipal Accountant-Designate,
MDRRMO – Designate and Municipal Budget Officers were required to submit
various reports such as RAAOs and Report on Sources and Utilizations since
none were submitted monthly to the Audit Team.
14.5. Of the reports required, the monthly Report on Sources and Utilizations of
LDRRMF was still not prepared and submitted by the concerned officers, thus,
transparency, timely provision of the information and accountability in the
utilization of the fund were not achieved.
14.6. We recommended that the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF
using the format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 should be
prepared monthly and certified correct by the Local Accountant and
submitted by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Audit Team.
14.7. During the Exit Conference, the Designated Municipal Accountant promised to
prepare the said monthly report which will be timely submitted by the Designated
– LDRRM Officer.

We recommended that the designated Municipal Accountant shall prepare
the necessary accounting entries to: (a.) record in the General Fund the
equipment purchased from LDRRMF Special Trust Fund under Disaster
Response and Rescue Equipment and adjust the Due to LGUs account, and;
(b.) record the corresponding accumulated depreciations for each equipment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant- Designate shall: (1)
prepare the necessary accounting entry to re-classify the ₱2,850,000.00
financial assistance as receivables under Due from Local Government Units
account, and (b) record the expense only when the Fund Utilization Report is
received from the barangays concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant- Designate, in the
preparation of succeeding Financial Statements, shall classify the current
portion of Loans Payable- Domestic as Current Liabilities and shall provide
detailed information as to the composition of each loan account in the Notes
to the Financial Statement.

We recommended that that the Municipal Budget Officer shall include in the
LGU’s annual budget the exact amount of loan amortizations due every year
on its existing loans based on the amortization schedule given by MDFO.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant –Designate shall send
a confirmation letter to MDFO before the end of each year and reconcile his
records with all existing loans with MDFO. Also, to make an official follow-
up to verify the status of the excess payment of the LGU.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer shall prepare the
documents for the payment of the loan amortizations at least one month
prior to the due dates so that LGU payments will be received by MDFO on
time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the Designated
MDRRMO, shall identify relevant and useful projects, programs and
activities in the LDRRMFIP that can be implemented during the year in
order to maximize the utilization of the 70% Mitigation Fund in accordance
with its intended purpose.

We also recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer, the Budget Officer
and the Accountant – Designate shall compute, before the end of each year,
the unused LDRRMF and transfer the amount to the Special Trust Fund
within the same year.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall use the
correct account code in recording transfer of unutilized LDRRMF to Trust
Fund.

We recommended that the Designated MDRRM Officer shall integrate in a
single LDRRMF Investment Plan the projects, programs and activities to be
funded from the unexpended Special Trust Fund and the current
appropriations of the 70% Mitigation Fund.

We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer shall:
(1) reconcile within the year the LGU records with the GSIS, and; (2)
communicate immediately to the said agency any changes in the employees’
salaries and employment status to update the records of the GSIS and tally
with the LGU records on premium contributions.

We recommended that the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) shall furnish
the payroll preparer immediately, preferably at the start of the month, with
copies of the GSIS billings to ensure that the correct amount of monthly
premium contributions and loan amortizations are deducted in the
employee’s salary in the payrolls.

We recommended that the personnel assigned to prepare the monthly
Payroll shall follow the new rates on PHIC premium contribution in
computing the personal and government shares of all regular employees.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate shall
review the amounts deducted in the payroll on monthly premium
contributions and reconcile the same with the required amount and the
amount to be remitted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall:
a) constitute and activate the Local Finance Committee which will update
the rates of taxes and fees provided in the current Tax Ordinance to
maximize the income generation capabilities of the LGU;
b) recommended to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval any proposed
revisions on the Tax Ordinance and implement the same; and
c) require each stallholders to sign a Contract of Lease upon application as
basis for the collection of rentals.

We recommended that the Municipal SWM Board, through the Municipal
Mayor as the Chairman, shall incorporate in the SWM Plan provision for
attendance of qualified LGU personnel in necessary trainings, preferably
those conducted by TESDA, who shall cascade his learnings to other
personnel involved in the collection of solid wastes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (12) instruct the MDRRMO to implement fully the activities in the LDRRMFIP in order to utilize the 70% mitigation fund in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and equip the LGU officials and its constituents the skill and knowledge to prepare and manage disasters.
observations.reason: Similar observation is included in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (13) Municipal Mayor (13) require the detailed DILG LGOO to refund immediately the Representation Allowance received in CY 2017. We also (14) recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer stop the payment of allowances which has no legal basis.

We recommend that management should (35) secure a copy of the purchaser’s permit from the National Telecommunication Commission and a dealer’s permit of the supplier issued also by the NTC to consider the transaction of purchasing Radio Handheld Transceivers, Systems and Accessories a valid and legitimate expenditure. Such supporting document is necessary to prevent transaction from being questioned in audit.

1.9   We recommended that the  Inventory  Committee  complete  the  physical
      count of LGU properties annually and prepare the  accurate  RPCPPE  to
      validate the amounts reported in the books.


1.10  We also recommended  that  the  designated  Municipal  Accountant  and
      designated General Service Officer reconcile their respective records,
      verify any discrepancies noted and prepare the  necessary  adjustments
      to present the accurate PPE account balances.

2.8   We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant-  Designate  prepare
      lapsing schedules for each infrastructure projects  to  determine  the
      correct Accumulated Depreciation as of December 31, 2018  and  prepare
      the necessary correcting entries.

3.6   We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:  (1)  conduct  an  updated
      inventory of all road  projects  of  the  LGU  and  prepare  a  report
      thereon, including the complete description and  cost  segregation  of
      road components for each road project, and (2) provide the  Designated
      Municipal Accountant and the Head of the General Services Office  with
      the Inventory Report.


3.7   Also, we recommended that  the  Municipal  General  Services  Officer-
      Designate prepare and maintain complete Local  Road  Network  Property
      Card for all the municipal roads and the Road Map of the LGU.


3.8   Lastly, we recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant-Designate  to
      keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for
      every road network to support the balance of the Road Network account.

4.9   We recommended that the  Local  Development  Council  through  the
      Municipal Planning and Development Officer include in the AIP projects
      which are ready for implementation during the budget year in order  to
      utilize fully the 20% Development Fund and provide socio-economic  and
      environmental progress to LGU residents.

5.10   We  recommended  that  the  MDRRMO-Designate  implement   fully   the
      activities in the LDRRMFIP in order to utilize the 70% mitigation fund
      in accordance  with  the  prescribed  guidelines  and  equip  the  LGU
      officials and its constituents the skill and knowledge to prepare  and
      manage disasters.  Also, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor  stop
      approving the payment for expenses sourced from the DRRMF unrelated to
      disaster preparedness, mitigation and prevention.

6.10  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through  the  Municipal  Solid
      Waste  Management  Board  and  the  Solid  Waste  Management  Officer-
      Designate, specifically undertake the following:


      a)    Allocate funds for  the  purchase  of  lot  for  an  operational
         Sanitary Landfill;


      b)   Implement feasible programs that supports re-use, recycling,  and
         composting activities;


      c)    Assist the 28 barangays in the establishment of their own MRF by
         ensuring that funds are appropriated for such purpose  in  the   CY
         2020 barangay budget;


      d)    Require all barangays to  segregate  solid  wastes  and  monitor
         closely the barangay’s compliance of its implementation; and


      e)     Disseminate  information  campaign  to  adopt  measures  toward
         effective solid waste management at the barangay level.

7.11  We recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor,  through  Human  Resource
      Management Officer:

           1. Fill  up  the  vacant  positions  in  the  LGU  plantilla  in
              accordance with CSC rules and regulations and subject  to  PS
              limitation;
           2. Reduce the number of Job Order Workers so that  functions  of
              the existing regular employees are not duplicated by JOs; and
           3. Pay the mandated daily minimum wage prescribed by  RTWPB  for
              Region VIII.

8.11  We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant-Designate  a.)  identify
      the Government’s Shares included in the ending balance as of  December
      31, 2018 and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry and b.) use
      the correct accounting entries from the  setting-up  of  salaries  and
      wages and salary deductions of regular employees up to the remittances
      and payments of monthly premium contributions.

9.9   We recommended that the Payroll Maker use the  latest  basic  salaries
      of each LGU personnel in computing the personal and government  shares
      as monthly premium contributions of all  regular  employees.  We  also
      recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate review the amounts
      deducted in the payroll as monthly premium contributions and reconcile
      them to the required  monthly  premium  contributions  to  detect  any
      difference and implement necessary corrections.

10.8  We recommended that  the  Municipal  Accountant-Designate  review  and
      reconcile monthly taxes  withheld  to  ensure  that  exact  amount  is
      remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) within the prescribed
      due date.

11.6  We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant-Designate:  a.)  prepare
      and file the necessary documents  for  the  registration  of  Mahaplag
      Inland Resort Hotel;  b.)  compute  the  taxes  due  to  the  National
      Government from the operations  of MIRH;  c.)  file  the  appropriate
      returns and pay the taxes due immediately and; d.)  file  the  returns
      and pay the taxes due in the succeeding periods.   Any  penalties  and
      surcharges should be  paid  by  the  employees  liable  for  the  late
      payment.

12.9  We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare the  separate
      Operating  Budget  for  the  Mahaplag  Inland  Resort  and  Hotel  and
      coordinate with the SB for its approval.  Also,  we  recommended  that
      all expenses pertaining to Mahaplag Inland Resort and Hotel, including
      the Personal Services, be sourced from the income generated in the
      Resort.


12.10 Further,  we  recommended  that  the  Sangguniang  Bayan  revisit  and
      evaluate the rates approved  in  2011  as  payment  for  the  services
      offered in Mahaplag Inland Resort and Hotel to increase the income  of
      the Resort.

We recommended that the (1) Municipal Mayor direct the designated Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer to continue and fast track the reconciliation of the balances of their respective records within the year. Request the assistance of other Department Heads to verify the discrepancies noted.
observations.reason: Similar observation is included in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (2) provide the necessary assistance to the Municipal Treasurer, in terms of manpower, in order to intensify and maximize collection of Real Property/Special Education Taxes. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer perform the following: (3) Develop an effective Tax Collection Campaign program to intensify and maximize collection of Real Property/Special Education Tax Receivables; (4) Deputize the Barangay Treasurers, in addition to the collectors of the LGU to collect all taxes on real property located in the Barangay, provided that the Barangay Treasurer is properly bonded for the purpose. Collection and remittance of RPT by Barangay Treasurer be closely monitored by the Municipal Treasurer. (5) Issue a notice of delinquency to each concerned property owner and post the same at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each Barangay of the Local Government Unit of Mahaplag; (6) Prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the Sanggunian concerned on or before December thirty-first (31st) of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection either by administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action; (7) We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the RPT/SET Receivables based on the report submitted by the Municipal Assessor.

We recommended that the (8) Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman, include in the Annual Investment Plan only projects with social, economic and environmental development outcomes which are investment or capital in nature so as to maximize the use of the 20% Development Fund and provide the citizens of the municipality with the benefits that the projects can provide.

We recommended that the Local School Board (10) maximize the utilization of the SEF for the improvement of educational facilities and other priority programs/projects/activities enumerated in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (15) require each program and project coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA and require them to submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents to the Source Agency immediately.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate and the Municipal Treasurer (16) remit to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) the full amount of taxes withheld and within the prescribed due date.
observations.reason: Similar observation is included in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (17) instruct the Head of the Human Resource and Management Office to reconcile their records with the GSIS and communicate immediately to the said agency any necessary change in the employees’ employment status to update the records of the GSIS and tally with the LGU records on premium contributions. We further recommended that the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) (18) furnish monthly all payroll preparer with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the correct amount of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations deducted in the payroll are based on the said GSIS billing. Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate (19) reconcile monthly the actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and against the amount remitted by the LGU to avoid discrepancies therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (20) provide the necessary assistance to the created GAD Focal Point System through the establishment of an Executive Committee and Technical Working Group, and (21) require them to participate actively in the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities so as to address gender issues and concerns in the locality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairman of the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC), (22) initiate the preparation of the following documents for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual Budget: a.) LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to needs for integration in the local development plan; b.) Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), and; c.) Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC). We also recommended that the (22) Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the total funds appropriated for programs, projects and activities to be undertaken by the different departments of the LGU which can be attributed for the strengthening of LCPC and children’s rights protection is equivalent to one percent (1%) of the IRA of the current year.

This is a reiterated finding, thus we repeatedly recommend (28) strict compliance with provisions of the law and rules governing cash advances. We reiterate compliance with the hereunder course of actions to wit: (29) a. A demand letter shall be made by the Municipal Accountant for the immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances outstanding as of December 31, 2016 from concerned accountable officers and employees including the suspension of salaries. Resort to legal action if evidences warrant shall also be made; (30) b. Strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as well as other related pertinent rules and regulations; and (31) c. Immediate submission to the audit team for audit of all original required documents on the settlement and liquidations of all accountable officers. (32) d. Further, we recommend that the LGU request authority from COA before salary deductions on instalment basis be applied.

We recommend that (33) prudence in the utilization and use of government funds be exercised and observed at all times by management to prevent incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, extravagant and unconscionable expenditures.

We recommend for (34) coursing of all procurements of common-use supplies to the PS-DBM and if without the required stocks, based on the PS Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks, the LGU may resort to alternative mode of procurement in accordance with Rule XVI of the revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should (36) ensure that all concerned officials and employees of the municipality to faithfully discharge their duties and functions for the submission of paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, collections receipts, deposits and its supporting documents within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommend for (37) management to strictly submit on the prescribed time all perfected purchase orders/contracts as well as IARs. This shall be complied with to facilitate releases of funds solely to valid transactions.

We recommend that management strictly cause the (38) preparation of the prescribed Annual Procurement Plan on the basis of which modes of acquisitions of supplies and materials are prescribed and carried out. This has to be complied with to ensure, among others, that procurements are necessary and at prices most advantageous to the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the designated Municipal
Accountant and General Service Officer to continue and fast track the
reconciliation of the balances of their respective records within the year. Request
the assistance of other Department Heads to verify the discrepancies noted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the necessary
assistance to the Municipal Treasurer, in terms of manpower, in order to intensify
and maximize collection of Real Property/Special Education Taxes.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer perform the
following:
1. Develop an effective Tax Collection Campaign program to intensify and
maximize collection of Real Property/Special Education Tax Receivables;
2. Deputize the Barangay Treasurers, in addition to the collectors of the LGU to
collect all taxes on real property located in the Barangay, provided that the
Barangay Treasurer is properly bonded for the purpose. Collection and
remittance of RPT by Barangay Treasurer be closely monitored by the
Municipal Treasurer.
3. Issue a notice of delinquency to each concerned property owner and post the
same at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in a publicly accessible
and conspicuous place in each Barangay of the Local Government Unit of
Mahaplag;
4. Prepare a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained
uncollected or unpaid for at least one (1) year, and a statement of the reason or
reasons for such non-collection or non-payment, and submit the same to the
Sanggunian concerned on or before December thirty-first (31st) of the year
immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred,
with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for
collection either by administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action;
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the RPT/SET
Receivables based on the report submitted by the Municipal Assessor.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal
Mayor as Chairman, include in the Annual Investment Plan only projects with social,
economic and environmental development outcomes which are investment or capital
in nature so as to maximize the use of the 20% Development Fund and provide the
citizens of the municipality with the benefits that the projects can provide.
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant -Designate appropriate and utilize the 20% Development Fund for
purposes identified in the AIP and those that are outlined in DILG-DBM Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Local School Board maximize the utilization of
the SEF for the improvement of educational facilities and other priority programs/
projects/activities enumerated in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor approve disbursements
from SEF only if it is in accordance with the provisions of the law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to
implement fully the activities in the LDRRMFIP in order to utilize the 70%
mitigation fund in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and equip the LGU
officials and its constituents the skill and knowledge to prepare and manage
disasters.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require each program and
project coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects
funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in
the MOA and require them to submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents
to the Source Agency immediately.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate and the
Municipal Treasurer remit to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) the full amount of taxes
withheld and within the prescribed due date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Head of the Human
Resource and Management Office to reconcile their records with the GSIS and
communicate immediately to the said agency any necessary change in the
employees’ employment status to update the records of the GSIS and tally with the
LGU records on premium contributions.
We further recommended that the Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) furnish
monthly all payroll preparer with copies of the GSIS billing to ensure that the
correct amount of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations deducted
in the payroll are based on the said GSIS billing.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant - Designate
reconcile monthly the actual deductions in the payroll against the GSIS Billing and
against the amount remitted by the LGU to avoid discrepancies therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the detailed DILG
LGOO to refund immediately the Representation Allowance received in CY 2017.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Budget Officer,
Accountant and Treasurer stop the payment of allowances which has no legal basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide the necessary assistance
to the created GAD Focal Point System through the establishment of an Executive
Committee and Technical Working Group, and require them to participate actively
in the implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities so as address
gender issues and concerns in the locality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor as the Chairman of the Local
Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC), initiate the preparation of the
following documents for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan and Annual
Budget: a.) LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan prioritized according to needs
for integration in the local development plan; b.) Local Development Plan for
Children (LDPC), and; c.) Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC).
We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the total
funds appropriated for programs, projects and activities to be undertaken by the
different departments of the LGU which can be attributed for the strengthening of
LCPC and children’s rights protection is equivalent to one percent (1%) of the IRA
of the current year.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the PPEs and require the General Services Officer-Designate/In-Charge to prepare and maintain a Property Cards of all PPEs and reconciled with the PPELC of the accounting unit.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The Municipal Accountant/ General Services Officer are in the process of reconciling their records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require each program and project coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in the MOA and require them to submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents to the Source Agency immediately.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Program/project coordinators were instructed to comply and submit required documents.

observations.recommendation: 1. We recommend that the Municipal Mayor revise the Memorandum of Agreement between the LGU and recipient barangays to include a requirement for the barangays to submit the Fund Utilization Report with the necessary attachments as soon as the intended program/project is implemented. 2. Also we recommend that the Municipal Mayor defer the grant of additional financial assistance unless the previous financial assistance have been liquidated.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Negotiation for the purchase of Lot for dumpsite is ongoing.
observations.reason: Negotiation for the purchase of Lot for dumpsite is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Mayor as Chairman, include in the Annual Investment Plan only environment and socio-development capital expenditures. Also mandate the Local Development Council to include in the AIP only capital expenditures for the development of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. LGU followed the timeline in the budget preparation calendar; PPAs were already incorporated in the Annual Budget of the LGU before the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 came out. The LGU’s officials rest assured that succeeding Annual Budget preparation, appropriation and utilization of the 20% DF will be in compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LDRRMIP be prepared annually and the Report on Sources and Utilization using the prescribed format be prepared and submitted on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Further, transfer the unobligated balance to the Special Trust Fund as of December 31.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant have prepared some plans and reports; transfer action for the unobligated balance was done.
observations.reason: The programs/projects were not specifically identified before the start of the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local School Board of Mahaplag, Leyte maximize the use of the SEF for the improvement of educational facilities and other priority programs/projects/activities enumerated in DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 s. 1998.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The LGU to ensure planned activities are prioritized; reiterated in this report.
observations.reason: This is reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Solid Waste Management Board to construct a Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) and provide necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRF and implement waste segregation and intensify information dissemination campaign.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Negotiation for the purchase of Lot for dumpsite is ongoing.
observations.reason: Negotiation for the purchase of Lot for dumpsite is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: A demand letter shall be made by the Municipal Accountant for immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances outstanding as of December 31, 2016 from concerned accountable officers and employees including the suspension of salaries. Resort to legal action if evidences warrant. Strict adherence to COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as well as other related pertinent rules and regulations; immediate submission to the audit team for audit of all original required documents on settlement and liquidations. Request authority from COA before salary deductions on instalment basis be applied.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Repeated recommendations and compliance actions have been undertaken.
observations.reason: Repeated findings; actions being undertaken to secure liquidation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend prudence in the utilization and use of government funds be exercised and observed at all times by management to prevent incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, extravagant and unconscionable expenditures.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Management has been advised to observe prudence in expenditures.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that procuring entities ensure compliance with the PS procurement process and secure PS Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks when applicable; resort to alternative mode of procurement in accordance with Rule XVI of the revised IRR of RA 9184 when necessary.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Procurements made coursing through required procedures where possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management, through its Focal Point Chairperson prepares and submits its GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval in conformity with the relevant provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No 2013-1 to ensure that all GAD related expenditures are aligned to the GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. The LGU to submit GAD Plan and Budget to DILG for review and approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the purchaser secure a copy of the purchaser’s permit from the National Telecommunications Commission and a dealer’s permit of the purchaser issued also by the NTC to consider the transaction a valid and legitimate expenditure. Such supporting document is necessary to prevent transaction from being questioned in audit.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Support documents to be secured and filed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should ensure that all concerned officials and employees of the municipality to faithfully discharge their duties and functions for the submission of paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, collections receipts, deposits and its supporting documents within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Officials were reminded to comply with submission timelines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend strict compliance with the prescribed timeframe for submission of contracts/purchase orders and notices of deliveries to facilitate audit verification and to ensure procurement documents are perfected.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented. Management to submit perfected procurement documents and IARs in the prescribed time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend preparation of the prescribed Annual Procurement Plan on the basis of which modes of acquisitions and procurements are carried out. Compliance to ensure procurements are necessary and at prices most advantageous to the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented. Preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan has been done as basis for procurement of supplies and materials.
observations.reason: Cause for preparation of the prescribed Annual Procurement Plan has been addressed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management, through its Focal Point Chairperson DILG, prepares and submits its GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval in conformity with the relevant provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No 2013-1 to ensure that all GAD related expenditures are aligned to the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor should: (1) strictly implement
the provisions of Sections 122 of PD 1445 by requiring the Inventory Committee
to conduct physical inventory-taking of all the PPEs of the LGU; (2) require the
General Services Officer-Designate/In-Charge to prepare and maintain a
Property Cards of all PPEs and have it reconciled with the PPELC of the
accounting unit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require each program and
project coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects
funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for
in the MOA and require them to submit the Liquidation Reports and other
documents to the Source Agency immediately.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor revise the Memorandum of
Agreement between the LGU and recipient barangays to include a requirement
for the barangays to submit the Fund Utilization Report with the necessary
attachments, as soon as the intended program/project is implemented.
Also, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor defer the grant of
additional financial assistance unless the previous financial assistance have been
liquidated.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor utilize the 20% Development
Fund only for the implementation of environmental, socio-economic development
projects. Also, mandate the Local Development Council to include in the AIP only
capital expenditures for the development of the municipality.

We recommend that the MDRRMO prepare the LDRRMFIP annually
using the prescribed format. Also, the Municipal Accountant-Designate prepare
the Report on Sources and Utilization using the prescribed format and submit it
to Audit Team on or before the 15thday after the end of each month. Further,
transfer the unobligated balance of P 1,988,084.72 as of December 31, 2016 to the
Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities-DRRM Fund”.

We recommend that Municipal School Board of Mahaplag, Leyte
maximize the use of the SEF for the improvement of educational facilities and
other priority programs/projects/activities enumerated in DECS-DBM-DILG
Joint Circular No. 1 s. 1998 to ensure that the desired benefits are availed by the
students.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board to construct Materials Recovery Facility (MRF).
We also recommend that the Mayor provide the necessary support in
enjoining the barangays to establish their own Materials Recovery Facility and
implement waste segregation. Also, intensify the information dissemination
campaign to adopt measures toward effective solid waste management at the
barangay level.

This is a 2015 reiterated AAR finding, thus we recommend strict compliance with provisions of the law and rules governing cash advances. We thus funds are exposed to loss or misuse. We reiterate compliance with hereunder 1 demand letters to officers
observations.managementAction: Send Partially Implement demand letters to officers employee made by the Municipal Accountant for the immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances outstanding as of December 31, 2016 from concerned accountable officers and employees including the suspension of salaries. Resort to legal action if evidences warrant shall also be made; Strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as well as other related pertinent rules and regulations; and Immediate submission to the audit team for audit of all original required documents on the settlement and liquidations of all accountable officers. Further, we recommend that the LGU request authority from COA before salary deductions on instalment basis be applied.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a JEV and make necessary adjustment of the said account pursuant to Section 63 of PD 1445
observations.managementAction: Prepared JEV No. 552-B dated 4/30/201? (text shows JEV prepared)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management utilizes its 20% development fund only for disbursements which are related to and/or in connection with the implementation of development project, programs and activities.
observations.reason: Implementing actions are ongoing as reflected by partial compliance with recommendation #3

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Provincial Accountant to strictly comply with the documentary requirements stipulated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in relation to the submission of contracts and disbursement documents.

We recommend that prudence in the utilization and use of government funds be exercised at all times by management to prevent incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, extravagant and unconscionable expenditures.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the stated provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 in controlling the use of government vehicles, to establish transparency, validity and propriety of the expenditures, and specifically undertake the following: a.) Instruct the concerned officers and employees of the agency to comply with the deficiencies noted on the stated transactions to avoid being suspended and disallowed in audit and; b.) Prepare and submit Monthly Summary of Fuel Consumption for each vehicle owned by the LGU supported with corresponding RIS for gasoline and duly accomplished drivers trip ticket.
