We recommend for coursing of all procurements of common-use supplies to the PS-DBM and if stocks are required based on the PS Certificate of Non-Availability of Stocks, the LGU may resort to alternative mode of procurement in accordance with Rule XVI of the revised IRR of RA 9184.
We recommend that management ensures the existence of a Memorandum of Agreement/Trust Agreement and resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan to the Barangay authorizing the transfer or receipt of funds.

We recommend that management should secure a copy of the purchaser’s permit from the National Telecommunications Commission and a dealer’s permit of the supplier issued by the NTC to consider the transaction of Handheld Transceivers, Systems and Accessories a valid and legitimate transaction. Such supporting document is necessary to prevent transaction from being questioned in audit.

We recommend that management should give priority in providing adequate office space and storeroom to the Audit Team for the safekeeping of its various documents submitted by LGU. The loss of any document has legal consequences and puts the agency at disadvantage.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should ensure that all concerned officials and employees of the municipality faithfully discharge their duties and functions for the prompt submission of paid disbursement vouchers, payroll, collection receipts, deposits and its supporting documents within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

In the grant, use and liquidation of cash advances, strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 as well as other pertinent rules shall be strictly observed; a. For those cash advances which remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2014, the Municipal Accountant shall issue a demand letter to the concerned accountable officers and employees for the liquidation of their cash advances. Failure to submit liquidation will be a ground for the suspension/withholding of salaries. Legal action should be resorted to if evidences warrant. b. Update regularly the Subsidiary Ledgers for all cash advances; and c. The Municipal Accountant should have a complete file of Disbursement Vouchers with complete supporting documents for all unliquidated cash advances, by labelling it as “Active Cash Advances”, which will support the Subsidiary Ledgers and the Aging of Cash Advances.

We recommend for strict compliance in the submission of Utilization Reports to the pertinent offices and the COA, in effect, capacitate the agency to protect the MDRRMF and make possible the assessment thru audit the propriety as well as the validity of disbursements made out of the funds.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: a. Convene the Solid Waste Management Board in order to integrate the various strategies in information dissemination and implementation of solid waste management plans of the barangays in its area of jurisdiction. b. Revise strategies in information dissemination on segregation of waste at source. c. Invite the active participation of the barangay officials in the information dissemination to their constituents on the proper segregation of the reusable or recyclable materials from the compostable waste to support the solid waste management program of the government; and d. Operationalize the lot intended for MRF so that proper segregation of mixed waste will be made and that only residual waste shall be transferred to the disposal facility which may eventually reduce the cost of waste disposal.

We recommend that management, through its GAD Focal Point Chairperson, thoroughly prepares an annual GAD plan and programs geared towards the achievement of the desired outcomes and goals as identified in the Framework Plan for Women and other pertinent regulations.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should ensure that all concerned officials and employees of the municipality faithfully discharge their duties and functions for the prompt submission of paid disbursement vouchers, payroll, collections receipts, deposits and its supporting documents within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 6 of COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommend strict adherence to the practice of checking as to complete documentations before claims are paid. Said measure must be carried out to preclude the suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit. On the other hand, all suppliers of medicines shall be required to submit necessary documentations as mandated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure that procured items are from legitimate sources in effect guaranty their effectiveness and genuineness.

We recommend for management to strictly cause the settlement or liquidation of cash advances to protect against the legal consequences of committing said deficiency including the questioning of the claims in audit. In addition, compliance with the specific measures is proposed; to wit: a. For those claims which remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2013, the Municipal Accountant shall demand for the liquidation from the concerned accountable officers and employees including the suspension of salaries. Resort to legal action if evidences warrant shall also be made; b. Update regularly the Subsidiary Ledgers for all cash advances; and c. The Municipal Accountant should have a complete file of Disbursement Vouchers with complete supporting documents for all unliquidated cash advances, by labelling it as “Active Cash Advances”, which will support the Subsidiary Ledgers and the Aging of Cash Advances. Considering the differing balances of records maintained for the account, the accountant shall be further directed to review said data and thereafter effect the required adjustments. This course of action shall be continually adhered to for records to be reflective of accurate data.

Specifically, the General Services Office shall be required to submit duly approved and properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets which should be serially numbered, and a summary of which shall be made at the end of the month in a Monthly Report of Official Travels, and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of government motor transportation. These are duly adhered for management to make possible proper monitoring of the agency’s consumption of fuels.

To achieve on-time submission of accounts, we recommend that the Treasurer and the Accountant be directed to promptly turn-over documents, reports and journals under their possession on the prescribed time which shall be duly covered by transmittal letters, in turn, cause their prompt submission for audit. Said transmittal letters shall prove the compliance of each to their duties over the agency’s accounts.

We recommend that whenever purchase of medicine shall be made, a certification from the requisitioning officer that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF should be attached to give assurance that the medicines to be procured are proven safe.

We recommend the following: a. In the grant, use and liquidation of cash advances, strict adherence to relevant provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 as well as other pertinent rules shall be strictly observed; b. For those cash advances which remained unliquidated as of December 31, 2012, the Municipal Accountant shall demand for the liquidation from the concerned accountable officers and employees including the suspension of salaries. Resort to legal action if evidences warrant shall also be made; c. Update regularly the Subsidiary Ledgers for all cash advances; and d. The Municipal Accountant should have a complete file of Disbursement Vouchers with complete supporting documents for all unliquidated cash advances, by labelling it as “Active Cash Advances”, which will support the Subsidiary Ledgers and the Aging of Cash Advances. Considering the differing balances of records maintained for the account, the accountant shall be further directed to review said data and thereafter effect the required adjustments. This course of action shall be continually adhered to for records to be reflective of accurate data.

We recommend that whenever purchases are made, the approved perfect copies of contracts and purchase orders as well as IARs shall be attached. This shall be complied with to facilitate payments solely to valid transactions.

We recommend that the management strictly cause the preparation of the prescribed Annual Procurement Plan on the basis of which modes of acquisitions of supplies and materials are to be carried out. This has to be complied with to ensure, among others, that procurements are necessary and at prices most advantageous to the municipality.

We recommend that the Mayor should direct the Municipal Accountant not to grant any cash advance to any employee with existing unliquidated cash advances and to ensure faithful compliance with the existing rules and regulations on the matter.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the concerned officials to formulate an annual GAD plan and programs geared towards the achievement of the desired outcomes and goals as identified in the Framework Plan for Women and other pertinent regulations.

We recommend that the concerned officials and employees be required to liquidate immediately their outstanding cash advances in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Accounting Circular No. 2006-001; otherwise withhold payment of any money due them in accordance with Section 37 of PD 1445.

Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to submit the paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, collection receipts, deposits and its supporting documents within the reglementary period, thereby preventing the audit team to conduct timely review and verification of paid transactions. Also provide adequate office space and storeroom to the Audit Team.

Require the Municipal Accountant/Treasurer to prepare an Annual Procurement Plan which shall include only those items crucial to the efficient discharge of Municipal functions pursuant to Sec. 4.7 of the IRR-A of RA 9184.
Require timely submission of documents and supporting records to enable audit verification and timely post-audit updates.
Prepare and submit annual GAD Plan and Budget for review and approval.

Strictly observe COA Circular No. 97-002 and related provisions; demand liquidation; update subsidiary ledgers and maintain complete files of disbursement vouchers to support subsidiary ledgers and aging schedules.
Ensure full compliance with documentary requirements prior to payments and require suppliers to submit necessary documentation as mandated under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
Ensure that the approved contracts/purchase orders and notices of deliveries are attached to payments and IARs for audit verification.
Management to take appropriate corrective actions including enforcing existing COA circulars, updating records, demanding liquidations, and improving procurement and monitoring processes.
